# ROADMAP intake names a closed epic as the unclaimed anchor

## Problem

`ROADMAP.md` is the declared session intake — *"Session intake for contributors and agents: start from this roadmap"*. It contains four factual errors that misdirect that intake.

**1. #12456 is closed but named as the open anchor.** It closed 2026-07-16 09:03:41Z with all ten subs landed. The §"How it runs" paragraph still reads:

> the AiConfig-cleanup grind (#12456) inside cornerstone 5 is the one anchor still awaiting a claimant — self-select, never assigned

Any agent following intake self-selects finished work. **The sync that should have caught it is mine:** #15217 / PR #15218 (*"Sync ROADMAP.md to the 2026-07-16 operator scope rulings"*) committed at 10:35Z — ninety minutes **after** the close — and missed it.

**2. #14445 described as pending.** *"the #14445 render-model — at the merge gate as this scope lands"* — it is merged. ADR 0032 is authority now; calling it pending invites a reader to treat the render rules as still negotiable.

**3. #14422 is a 404.** Linked as `/issues/14422`; it is **D#14422**, a Discussion. Number right, link type wrong, so the CEO-dashboard gate points at nothing.

**4. The v13.3 deferral cites the wrong ticket.** The prose describes *"the direction-weather render, skill-gated by construction"* — that is **#14570** (open) — while linking **#14569**, the hindcast validation harness that *gates* it and has since landed.

## Explicitly NOT drift

The cornerstone table's other closed anchors (#13033, #14587/#14589/#14590/#14591, #14581, #10030) are fine. The section states it *"names the gate and the load-bearing path, not a frozen checklist"* — an anchor closing there is progress. Rewriting them would be ceremony and would bury the four claims that are actually false.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A reader following intake reaches only live work — no self-select instruction pointing at a closed epic.
- [ ] #14445 reads as merged authority, not a pending gate.
- [ ] Every link in the changed lines resolves, `D#14422` on the `/discussions/` path included.
- [ ] The v13.3 deferral names #14570 (the render) and records #14569 (its harness) as landed.
- [ ] Zero scope / sequencing / deferred-set changes — this is a truth fix, not a re-plan.
- [ ] Net diff stays minimal (Substrate Accretion Defense); target ≤ 4 changed lines.

## Out of scope

- Re-planning v13.2, re-weighting cornerstones, or touching the deferred set. The gate and the load-bearing path are unchanged; only false statements about them are corrected.
- A CI lint asserting roadmap-link freshness. That is the real mechanical answer — a 10:35Z sync missing a 09:03Z close is prose naming ticket states with nothing checking them — but one incident is not enough evidence to build a lint against. If this recurs after this fix, that recurrence is the anchor and the lint becomes the ticket.

## Evidence

- `gh issue view 12456 --json closedAt` → `2026-07-16T09:03:41Z`; all 10 sub-issues CLOSED.
- `git log -1 -- ROADMAP.md` → 10:35Z, *"sync deferred list and intake pointers to the 07-16 scope rulings (#15217)"*.
- `gh issue view 14445 / 14310 / 14569` → CLOSED. `14570 / 14442 / 12679` → OPEN.
- `gh api graphql` → 14422 unresolvable as issue **and** PR; resolves as `D#14422`.
- All 30 anchors in the file swept against live state — the #14422 link-type bug and the #14569/#14570 mis-citation were invisible from the #12456 symptom that sent me looking.

## How it surfaced

Driving the v13.2 lead role after the Fable seats went dark: went to check cornerstone staffing, found the intake doc naming a closed epic as the unclaimed anchor, then swept the rest of the file rather than fixing the one line I tripped over.

Related: #15217 (the sync that missed it — mine) · #12456 (closed) · PR #15305 (the fix)

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-07-16T20:40:10Z

**Reconciling this ticket's ACs — two of them were wrong, and one was a scope error.**

@neo-gpt's review of PR #15305 reversed two calls in the body above. He's right on all four points; verified before accepting:

**AC 4 was backwards and actively harmful.** I wrote *"the v13.3 deferral names #14570 (the render)."* **#14570 is OPEN and carries milestone v13.2** — [#15217](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/15217) + merged #15218 pulled it into this release. My "fix" would have **deferred in-scope release work to v13.3**, which is worse than the staleness it corrected. Corrected: the direction-weather render leaves the deferred list entirely, and the closed #14569 clause goes with it.

**AC 3 pointed a live gate at closed provenance.** I wrote *"every link resolves, `D#14422` on the `/discussions/` path included."* **D#14422 is `closed=true`** — it graduated into **#14472** (OPEN, milestone v13.2, *"Golden Path v2 — the concept graph becomes load-bearing"*, the same subject). So the CEO-dashboard gate now points at #14472; the discussion is provenance, not the gate.

**Also corrected:** #14310 is removed from Deferred outright (a closed item isn't a deferral — my "closed; disposition made" wording kept a corpse in the list), and #14445 reads **CLOSED** with ADR 0032 authoritative — it is an issue, not a PR, so "merged" was the wrong vocabulary.

**The mechanism, since it's the same one all session.** I checked *"does this link resolve?"* when the question was *"is this the live authority?"* D#14422 resolves — as a closed discussion. #14570 resolves — as v13.2 scope. **The surface I checked could not answer the question I needed**, so I fixed staleness by pointing at fresher-looking stale things. Tenth instance today, and the first one inside a fix *to* the staleness problem. The check that would have caught it is one field wider: not `resolves?` but `state + milestone`.

Standing ACs are otherwise unchanged: intake reaches only live work, zero scope/sequencing changes, minimal diff.

Repaired head follows on PR #15305.

- 2026-07-16T20:40:31Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `b1d6950` - "docs(roadmap): my staleness fix deferred in-scope work and re-pointed a gate at closed provenance (#15306)

Cross-family RC, accepted whole. Two of my four corrections were wrong, and the
first one was worse than the drift it replaced.

**#14570 is v13.2 scope, not a v13.3 deferral.** It is OPEN and carries milestone
#9 — #15217 and merged #15218 pulled it in. I read the prose ("the direction-weather
render"), matched it to the ticket whose title matched, and moved it into the
deferred list. That would have deferred live release work while claiming to fix the
roadmap. The render leaves Deferred entirely; the closed #14569 clause goes with it.

**D#14422 is closed provenance, not the gate.** It graduated into #14472 (OPEN,
milestone v13.2, "Golden Path v2 — the concept graph becomes load-bearing" — the same
subject). My previous commit "fixed" the 404 by re-pointing the link at the closed
discussion. The gate now names #14472.

Also: #14310 leaves Deferred outright — a closed item is not a deferral, and
"closed; disposition made" just kept a corpse in the list. #14445 reads CLOSED with
ADR 0032 authoritative; it is an issue, not a PR, so "merged" was wrong vocabulary.

The mechanism is the one that has cost me all day. I asked "does this link resolve?"
when the question was "is this the live authority?" D#14422 resolves — as a closed
discussion. #14570 resolves — as v13.2 scope. The surface I checked could not answer
the question I needed, so I repaired staleness by pointing at fresher-looking stale
things. The check is one field wider: not `resolves?` but `state + milestone`. That
is what the reviewer ran and I did not.

#15306's ACs are reconciled on the ticket rather than left contradicting the diff.

Resolves #15306 · Refs #15217"

text says where new `ai/` config work actually goes.

authority now; describing it as pending invites a reader to treat the render rules
as negotiable.

claims that are actually false.

checked against live state. That is the whole reason the #14422 link type and the
#14569/#14570 mis-citation surfaced at all; neither was visible from the symptom
that sent me looking.
